task_completion.description: Create required layers with specific colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the LibreCAD Layer List panel, there should be layers named 'OUTLINE' (White/Black depending on theme), 'FRETS' (Red), 'INLAYS' (Cyan), and 'CENTERLINE' (Yellow).
A yellow horizontal line running through the middle of the drawing area.
A white (or black) tapered rectangular shape that is narrower on the left side (nut) and wider on the right side (heel).
22 red vertical lines crossing the fretboard outline. The spacing between the lines should progressively decrease from left to right.

task_completion.description: Draw inlay circles at specified frets
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cyan circles placed in the spaces between frets. Single circles on the centerline for frets 3, 5, 7, 9, 15, 17, 19, and 21. Two circles vertically aligned (above and below the centerline) at the 12th fret.

task_completion.description: Save the template as a DXF file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar or the command line history at the bottom shows the file is saved as 'fretboard_template.dxf'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application interface being actively used, rather than a terminal window running a script to generate the DXF file.
The command line interface at the bottom of the LibreCAD window shows evidence of tool usage and coordinate entries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 22 fret positions (36.37mm to 466.16mm) match a 648mm (25.5-inch) scale length. Nut width is 43mm, heel width is 56mm. Inlay pattern is standard for 22 frets (3, 5, 7, 9, 12, 15, 17, 19, 21), with the 12th fret having two dots at Y=12 and Y=-12. Inlay diameter is 6mm.
